summary: Persistent backdoor with obfuscated remote-executed payload and HWID exfiltration
details: On load, launcher.js (the package main and bin entry) establishes Windows autorun persistence by writing HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\OneDriveUpdate to a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that silently relaunches `node.exe launcher.js` via wscript with hidden window. The Run-key name and VBS filename impersonate Microsoft OneDrive, and launcher.js sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` to masquerade as the legitimate Windows RuntimeBroker.exe. launcher.js then eval()s server.obf.js, an 81KB obfuscator.io-packed file (rotating string array + RC4-style decoder) whose decoded behavior derives a hardware fingerprint by reading HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell, the OS hostname, and the C: volume serial, SHA256s them, and POSTs `{key, hwid, nonce, app, version}` to a runtime-decoded LICENSE_URL. A bundled 6.8MB sens.node native addon containing strings such as 'Aimbot', 'Triggerbot', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head' is loaded and exposed via socket.io start/stop handlers driven by a public/index.html cheat UI. Installing or loading this package establishes durable, attributable persistence on the host, executes opaque attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges, and exfiltrates a stable hardware identifier to an obfuscated remote endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
